title: codex-hooks.v1 — the engine's Codex hook registration file (.codex/hooks.json, whole file)
description: The whole-file contract for the repository-level Codex hook registration the engine commits. The file shape is Codex's own: a top-level `hooks` object keyed by lifecycle event, each event holding a list of matcher groups, each group holding a list of command hooks. This schema pins the event keys to the closed set the Codex platform documents (SessionStart, SubagentStart, PreToolUse, PermissionRequest, PostToolUse, PreCompact, PostCompact, UserPromptSubmit, SubagentStop, Stop) so a typo'd event cannot silently register nothing, and requires every hook to be a command hook with a non-empty command. It deliberately does NOT pin which events the engine binds, the matcher vocabulary, or the command form — the exact engine matrix (its mirror of the Claude registration, the deliberately absent plan-acceptance hook, the command grammar) is the provider-parity check's domain and the hook-command drift test's, so the registration policy lives in one leg rather than a schema that would have to change with every wiring edit. Per-hook platform extras (statusMessage, command_windows, timeouts, and whatever Codex adds next) pass through openly. Enforced live by the codex-hooks-schema check (a validators-core schema rule) via an explicit rule-level schema override, because this file is a wiring target, not a catalogued surface (eADR-0034).

hooks.description: The registration body, keyed by Codex lifecycle event. Only documented platform events are legal keys.

.*.description: The matcher groups registered on this event.

matcher.description: The platform's event filter (a tool-name pattern on PreToolUse, a session source such as startup/resume/clear on SessionStart). Omitted means the group fires on every occurrence of the event.

type.description: The hook mechanism. The engine registers command hooks only.

command.description: The shell command Codex runs for this hook.
